name: Southwest Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1 small baking potato, peeled and diced
1/4 red bell pepper, seeded and diced
1/4 yellow bell pepper, seeded and diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Shuck corn, remove silk, and cut kernels from the cob.
2. Set aside 3/4 cup of corn kernels.
3. Heat oil in a heavy saucepan over medium heat. Stir in the remaining corn kernels and onions. Saute for about 10 minutes or until onions are soft and juices have cooked down.
4. Add garlic, 1 chopped poblano pepper, 1 chopped serrano chili, and chicken stock. Bring to a boil, stirring frequently.
5. Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Stir in cream. Return soup to a simmer and cook for about 5 minutes or until slightly reduced.
7. Remove from heat. Pour soup into a blender and blend until very smooth. Season to taste with salt and lime juice.
8. Fill a large pot three-quarters full with water. Bring to a boil. Season lightly with salt.
9. Add potato and cook for 2 minutes.
10. Add reserved 3/4 cup of corn kernels and cook for 2 minutes.
11. Add jicama, bell peppers, and remaining diced poblano and serrano chilies; cook for 2 minutes.
12. Drain vegetables and immediately fold into warm soup.
13. Pour equal portions into six warm soup bowls. Sprinkle an equal amount of chopped cilantro on each and serve immediately.
The entire soup may be made up to one day in advance. Reheat, but do not cook, just before serving.
